questions.questionText: Which 3D shape has all 6 faces as equal squares?
questions.explanation: A cube has 6 faces, and all of them are equal squares. A dice is a good example of a cube.
hints: Think of a dice
All faces look exactly the same

questions.questionText: How many faces does a cuboid have?
questions.explanation: A cuboid has exactly 6 faces. All faces are rectangles. Think of a brick or a tiffin box!
hints: A cuboid looks like a box
Count top, bottom, front, back, left, right

questions.questionText: Which 3D shape looks like a ball?
questions.explanation: A sphere is perfectly round like a ball. A cricket ball, a marble, and a globe are all spheres.
hints: It is perfectly round from every side
It can roll in any direction

questions.questionText: A tin can (like a Bournvita container) is shaped like a:
questions.explanation: A tin can is shaped like a cylinder. It has 2 flat circular faces (top and bottom) and 1 curved surface.
hints: It has circles on top and bottom
It can roll sideways

questions.questionText: How many edges does a cube have?
questions.explanation: A cube has 12 edges. There are 4 edges on top, 4 edges on the bottom, and 4 vertical edges connecting them.
hints: Count edges on top, bottom, and sides separately
An edge is where two faces meet

questions.questionText: How many vertices (corners) does a cuboid have?
questions.explanation: A cuboid has 8 vertices. There are 4 corners on the top face and 4 corners on the bottom face.
hints: Count corners on top and bottom
A cube also has the same number of vertices

questions.questionText: True or False: A sphere has no edges and no vertices.
questions.explanation: True! A sphere is perfectly round with only 1 curved surface. It has 0 edges and 0 vertices. That is why a ball rolls so easily!
hints: Think of a cricket ball - can you find any corners or sharp lines?

questions.questionText: Which 3D shape has exactly 1 vertex (one pointed tip)?
questions.explanation: A cone has exactly 1 vertex at its pointed tip. Think of an ice cream cone or a birthday party cap!
hints: Think about which shape has a pointed top
An ice cream shape

questions.questionText: Which of these is NOT a 3D shape?
questions.explanation: A circle is a 2D (flat) shape. It has no height or depth. Cube, cone, and cylinder are all 3D shapes.
hints: One of these is flat and cannot be held
3D shapes have length, width, AND height

questions.questionText: How many flat faces does a cylinder have?
questions.explanation: A cylinder has 2 flat circular faces - one on the top and one on the bottom. It also has 1 curved surface going around the middle.
hints: Think of a tin can - are the top and bottom flat?
The circles on top and bottom are flat faces

questions.questionText: What shape do you get if you trace the bottom of a glass (cylinder) on paper?
questions.explanation: The bottom of a glass is circular, so tracing it gives you a circle. The flat face of a cylinder is always a circle!
hints: What is the shape of the bottom of a glass?
Place a glass upside down and draw around it

questions.questionText: A right angle looks like the shape of the letter:
questions.explanation: A right angle looks like the letter L. You can find right angles at the corners of squares, rectangles, books, and doors.
hints: Think about the corner of your notebook
It is the angle at the corner of a square

questions.questionText: Which 3D shape can roll on a flat surface?
questions.explanation: A sphere can roll easily because it is curved all around. A cube and a cuboid have only flat faces, so they slide but do not roll.
hints: Think about what happens when you push a ball versus a box
Flat faces do not roll

questions.questionText: True or False: A cone has 2 edges.
questions.explanation: False! A cone has only 1 curved edge where the flat circular base meets the curved surface. It does not have 2 edges.
hints: Think about where the flat and curved parts meet on an ice cream cone

questions.questionText: Which object is shaped like a cuboid?
questions.explanation: A matchbox is shaped like a cuboid. It has 6 rectangular faces. A cricket ball and marble are spheres, and a bangle is like a ring (torus).
hints: Think about which one looks like a box
It has flat rectangular sides

questions.questionText: How many vertices does a cylinder have?
questions.explanation: A cylinder has 0 vertices. Its edges are curved circles, so there are no corners where edges meet.
hints: Think of a tin can - can you find any sharp corners?
Its edges go around in circles

questions.questionText: Where can you find a right angle in a classroom?
options: At the corner of the blackboard
questions.explanation: The blackboard has rectangular corners, and each corner is a right angle. Balls, circles, and round clocks are curved and do not have right angles.
hints: Right angles are found at corners of rectangles and squares
Which option has straight edges meeting?

questions.questionText: A cube and a cuboid both have the same number of:
questions.explanation: Both a cube and a cuboid have 6 faces, 12 edges, and 8 vertices. The difference is that a cube has all equal square faces, while a cuboid has rectangular faces.
hints: Compare the properties of both shapes
They are closely related shapes
